![Image 1: Loading video](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/1zJWirDS63o/sddefault.jpg#404_is_fine)
AI Decoded speaks to leading tech podcaster Scott Galloway about the growing controversy surrounding Anthropic's latest AI model, Claude Mythos.

During testing, the system uncovered thousands of hidden vulnerabilities in the software underpinning most of the world's computers — prompting Anthropic to decide it was too dangerous to release.

The company says Mythos can outperform humans at certain hacking and cyber‑security tasks, raising fresh concerns among regulators, lawmakers and financial institutions about the risks to global digital infrastructure.

Also on the show, we reveal how US students are changing their majors at university in order to 'AI proof' their future careers and how nearly half of Gen Z would like to turn back the clock to the late 90's before AI was even a thing.

Joining Christian Fraser is the BBC's AI correspondent Marc Cieslak and AI Decoded co-host Priya Lakhani.

Markdown Content:
Advertisement

[SKIP ADVERTISEMENT](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/01/business/dealbook/openai-anthropic-compute.html#after-top)

Image

![Image 1](https://static01.nyt.com/images/2020/04/21/multimedia/THE-MORNING-SECTION-BREAK/THE-MORNING-SECTION-BREAK-articleLarge.jpg?quality=75&auto=webp&disable=upscale)

Image

![Image 2: Sam Altman of OpenAI, in a dark suit and blue shirt and tie, seen through a courthouse window.](https://static01.nyt.com/images/2026/05/01/multimedia/01db-altman-oai-fcbj/01db-altman-oai-fcbj-articleLarge.jpg?quality=75&auto=webp&disable=upscale)

Sam Altman, right, has suggested that OpenAI, when releasing its latest A.I. model, would take a different approach than its rival, Anthropic, did. Credit...Brennan Smart for The New York Times

**Andrew here.** The [conversation we began](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/30/business/dealbook/powell-fed-trump-warsh.html) on Thursday about Anthropic’s potential computing constraints is continuing, as OpenAI unveils a new model that supporters say doesn’t suffer from a lack of resources. We look at what that might mean for businesses, customers and national security.

Also, Apple blew away earnings expectations. But as it faces questions about its A.I. strategy, the company announced a new plan that could give it more cash to invest in the technology. More below.

## The compute question

Earlier this week, many in the technology industry were asking whether OpenAI was in danger of falling behind in the artificial intelligence race. But the release of the company’s new model may change its fortunes — and is contributing to a wider debate about the importance of computing resources.

**Meet GPT-5.5-Cyber.** The mouthful of a name is [OpenAI’s newest cybersecurity model](https://x.com/sama/status/2049712078836170843), which Sam Altman, the company’s C.E.O., said on Thursday would be released to “critical cyber defenders” in a few days.

Altman said OpenAI would work “with the entire ecosystem and the government” to figure out how to quickly roll out wider access for the model in the name of safeguarding global computing systems. “We want to rapidly help secure companies/infrastructure,” he added.

**It was an implicit rebuke to his rival Anthropic,** even as Altman is [doing something similar](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/after-dissing-anthropic-for-limiting-mythos-openai-restricts-access-to-cyber-too/). Anthropic has restricted access to its Claude Mythos Preview model to several dozen companies and a growing number of U.S. federal agencies, arguing that Mythos’s ability to ferret out cybersecurity threats was a potential weapon that could be easily abused.

https://www.axios.com/2026/05/01/washing.title: Washington has a new Anthropic problem
https://www.axios.com/2026/05/01/washing.text:
URL Source: https://www.axios.com/2026/05/01/washington-new-anthropic-problem

Published Time: 2026-05-01T09:10:05.419270Z

Markdown Content:
Anthropic is both a risk and a necessity to [AI](https://www.axios.com/technology/automation-and-ai) progress, at least in the White House's telling.

**Why it matters:** That tension is shaping AI policy in real time, as the White House [realizes](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/29/trump-anthropic-pentagon-ai-executive-order-gov) it needs the company it's been fighting.

**Driving the news:** The White House is inching toward welcoming Anthropic back into the government fold after months of [animosity](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/27/anthropic-pentagon-supply-chain-risk-claude) and [legal](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/09/anthropic-sues-pentagon-supply-chain-risk-label) battles with the [Pentagon](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/13/ai-policy-power-center-pentagon-anthropic) because its most advanced models are too powerful to ignore.

**The big picture:**The Trump administration's [goal](https://www.axios.com/pro/tech-policy/2025/07/23/white-house-releases-ai-action-plan) on AI has been to be as hands-off and pro-innovation as possible. But as models get more powerful, that stance is breaking down.

*   Washington is stepping in, shaping policy around who gets access to the most advanced systems and how they're deployed, driven by growing urgency over what the technology can do.

**Flashback:**The standoff started earlier this year when talks broke down over how the Pentagon could use Anthropic's AI in classified settings.

*   That led to public spats, lawsuits, new deals struck with other frontier AI companies, and the unprecedented move to label Anthropic as a supply chain risk, a designation usually reserved for foreign adversaries.
*   The White House at one point considered an executive order meant to weed out Anthropic from government systems entirely, as Axios previously [reported](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/09/trump-white-house-anthropic-executive-order).

**Yes, but:** The government couldn't ice Anthropic out for long.

*   That realization sank in as its powerful model [Mythos](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-mythos-preview-cybersecurity-risks) rolled out and agencies — despite the Pentagon spat — started [testing](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/19/nsa-anthropic-mythos-pentagon) it along with other AI companies' most advanced cyber models.
*   As the Pentagon and Anthropic continued to battle in court, the White House kicked off a [thaw](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/17/anthropic-white-house-wiles-bessent-amodei) with the company.

**What they're saying:**"When you're regulating by contract, it's basically creating a huge amount of power in the agency that's negotiated that contract and then becomes effectively the de facto policy of the administration," Jessica Tillipman, associate dean for government procurement law studies at George Washington University, told Axios.

# Musk Admits Distilling OpenAI Data For His XAI—Here’s Why That’s Controversial

Published 2 days ago

[Forbes](https://www.forbesafrica.com/author/forbes)

Antonio Pequeño IV

![Image 3: Musk Admits Distilling OpenAI Data For His XAI—Here’s Why That’s Controversial](https://cms.forbesafrica.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/GettyImages-2216318870.jpg)

(Photo by Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images)

## Topline

Elon Musk said Thursday his artificial intelligence startup, xAI, has used technology from OpenAI to train its own AI models, a process commonly called distilling, making the acknowledgment as he was cross-examined in his lawsuit accusing the rival AI firm of abandoning its promise of a nonprofit structure by switching to a profit-driven model.

[**_This was first published on Forbes.com_**](https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2026/04/30/elon-musk-admits-xai-distilled-openai-data-to-train-models-heres-what-that-means/)

## Key Facts

An OpenAI attorney asked Musk if xAI ever “distilled” technology from OpenAI, to which Musk responded, “Generally A.I. companies distill other A.I. companies,” according to[The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/04/30/technology/openai-trial-sam-altman-elon-musk/f16377a7-9d85-5498-aa36-fd451c14917e?smid=url-share).

Advertisement

Distillation involves using outputs from a larger AI model to train a smaller one, and Musk said xAI “partly” used OpenAI’s technology to train its own AI models.

OpenAI’s terms of service prohibit outputs from being used to train competing AI models.

May 02, 2026 10:00:00
# X restructures its advertising platform, introducing the latest AI models in the largest system overhaul in the company's history.

This article, originally posted in **[Japanese](http://gigazine.net/news/20260502-x-ai-powered-ad-platform/)** on 10:00 May 02, 2026, may contains some machine-translated parts.

If you would like to suggest a corrected translation, please click **[here](javascript:iform.submit();)**.

 In 2026, the 20th anniversary of the founding of Twitter, the predecessor to X, X announced that it would be 'rebuilding its most ambitious advertising platform since its inception.'

**X user Business: 'X launches the most ambitious advertising platform rebuild in its 20-year history' / X**

**[https://x.com/XBusiness/status/2049843732141256979](https://x.com/XBusiness/status/2049843732141256979)**

**X announces a rebuilt ad platform powered by AI | TechCrunch**

**[https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/x-announces-a-rebuilt-ad-platform-powered-by-ai/](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/x-announces-a-rebuilt-ad-platform-powered-by-ai/)**

**X introduces rebuilt AI-powered ad platform | Social Media Today**

**[https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/x-introduces-rebuilt-ai-powered-ad-platform/819029/](https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/x-introduces-rebuilt-ai-powered-ad-platform/819029/)**

 According to X, the rebuilt ad manager is designed to help advertisers launch better campaigns faster and achieve a higher ROI (Return on Investment).

 The three main pillars are as follows:

**Simplicity**

 Our intuitive AI-driven platform makes creating campaigns easy and accurate.

 - **Unparalleled ease of management**

 We provide advertisers with complete operability and speed.

 • **Excellent AI-powered performance**

 We deliver superior results, faster optimization, more precise targeting, and AI-powered relevance.

 This upgrade introduces a cutting-edge AI-powered search and ranking system to X Ads, enabling more accurate, relevant, and dynamic ad delivery that deeply understands user behavior and adapts to what's happening on X in real time.

 Furthermore, the shift to AI-driven contextual and semantic advertising is expected to improve relevance, engagement, and ROI.

 Over the next few months, advertisers will be offered a sophisticated, modern design, along with further improvements, new features, and more advanced functionalities.
